commit.message:
Fix remote TUI input: default to bracketed paste when detection fails

The remote /send API wrote raw text to the FIFO but copilot TUI
sessions silently dropped it. Root cause: _check_tui_session()
returned False when the log file didn't exist (session not created
via shell_wrapper), so bracketed paste was never applied.

Fix: default to bracketed paste (True) when TUI detection fails —
no log file, empty log, or any exception. Bracketed paste is harmless
for shell sessions (ignored or treated as printable) but critical for
TUI apps that use focus-based input routing.

This enables controlling copilot sessions on dandroid1 via zpilot MCP
tools instead of requiring Playwright browser automation.

commit.message:
feat: Timeseries Loader (#137)

# New Sub Command for Time Series

Takes in a ts group or tsid(s) and copies the data from one CDA instance
to another

`cwms-cli timeseries data -h`
gives
```bash
Usage: cwms-cli load timeseries data [OPTIONS]

  Copy timeseries data for a single location in a target CDA from a source CDA.

Options:
  -v, --verbose                   Increase verbosity (repeat for more detail).
  --dry-run / --no-dry-run        Show what would be written without storing to target.  [default: no-dry-run]
  --target-api-key TEXT           Target API key (if required by the target CDA).
  --target-cda TEXT               Target CWMS Data API root. Default: http://localhost:8081/cwms-data/  [required]
  --source-office TEXT            Source office ID (e.g. SWT, SWL).  [required]
  --source-cda TEXT               Source CWMS Data API root. Default: https://cwms-data.usace.army.mil/cwms-data/  [required]
  --ts-id TEXT                    ID of the timeseries to copy (e.g. 'LocID.*').
  --ts-group TEXT                 ID of the timeseries group to copy (e.g. 'GroupID.*').
  --ts-group-category-id TEXT     ID of the timeseries group category to copy.
  --ts-group-category-office-id TEXT
                                  ID of the timeseries group category office to copy.
  --begin [%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z|%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S %Z]
                                  Start date for timeseries data (e.g. '2022-01-01T00:00:00-06:00').
  --end [%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z|%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S %Z]
                                  End date for timeseries data (e.g. '2022-01-31T00:00:00-0600').
  -h, --help                      Show this message and exit.
```



This pull request introduces a new CLI command for copying timeseries
data between CWMS Data Access (CDA) instances, along with several
supporting improvements and fixes. The main focus is on enabling users
to copy timeseries data for a single location or a group of timeseries,
with robust input validation and dry-run support. Additional changes
include dependency updates and minor bug fixes.

**New timeseries data copy functionality:**

* Added a new `data` subcommand to the `timeseries` CLI group, allowing
users to copy timeseries data for a specific timeseries ID or a
timeseries group from a source CDA to a target CDA, with options for
specifying time range and group/category details. Input validation
ensures correct option usage. (`cwmscli/load/timeseries/timeseries.py`,

commit.message:
feat(resilience): add retry utilities and circuit breaker (#62)

Implement shared retry decorator factory (tenacity) and async circuit
breaker with sliding-window failure tracking per S23 §3.3-3.4.

Retry (src/tta/resilience/retry.py):
- RetryConfig frozen dataclass with per-service presets
- 6 presets: DATABASE, REDIS, NEO4J, LLM_CALL, LLM_GENERATION, WORLD_GRAPH
- with_retry decorator: exponential backoff + jitter (AC-23.4)
- Exhaustion raises AppError with correct category (FR-23.11)

Circuit Breaker (src/tta/resilience/circuit_breaker.py):
- Sliding window via deque[float] of monotonic timestamps
- closed → open → half-open state machine (FR-23.13)
- Per-service config presets: LLM, PG, NEO4J, REDIS (FR-23.14)
- Single-probe guard prevents concurrent half-open probes
- Exponential cooldown for flapping, capped at 5min (EC-23.6)
- Exception filter: counted_exceptions controls which types trip
- State changes logged at WARN (FR-23.15)

Tests: 38 tests (24 CB + 14 retry), all passing.
Composition: CB wraps retry — circuit-open raises SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
which is NOT retryable, so it fails fast.

Closes #62

commit.message:
fix: always inject user profile into system prompt

The engine was only initialized (with system prompt) when isReady=false.
If the user set their profile after the engine was already running, it was
never picked up.

Fix:
- Add updateSystemPrompt() to InferenceEngine interface — recreates the
  conversation with a new system prompt without reloading the model (cheap)
- ChatViewModel.initializeConversation() now always builds the full system
  prompt (profile + datetime). If engine is ready it calls updateSystemPrompt,
  otherwise it initializes as before.

This means every time a new chat is opened, the latest profile is applied.

  despite highest TIR, not just 'best-controlled'
- Fix ISF→TBR claim: ISF ratio >1.5× doesn't universally predict
  higher TBR; d(0.7%) and e(1.8%) have low TBR due to AID compensation

EXP-1523 flag agreement rates, EXP-1524 drift correlation (r=0.825)
and direction agreement (100%), EXP-1526 excursion correlation
(r=-0.169), EXP-1527 Jaccard (0.49), EXP-1528 learning curve,
EXP-1510 bootstrap (94.5%/81.2%), all prior experiment cross-refs
(EXP-1301/1309/1334/1341), grade thresholds, code constants.
